checks.finding: T-001 through T-005 correctly classify integrity-boundary and gate-bypass risks as high with tdd; T-006 correctly classifies reversible release metadata as medium with acceptance-first.

Every high-risk task requires Red-to-Green logs, independent review, spec_revision, environment, changed-file hashes, requirement-traceability proof, and exercised rollback evidence. No critical task is declared.
T-001 through T-006 each define one cohesive deliverable with bounded planned files, explicit scope, inspectable completion criteria, and exclusions.

The task plan covers malformed and unreadable inputs, stale and forged provenance, traversal and symlink escape, legacy overbreadth, predecessor inversion, lifecycle bypass, LF/CRLF parity, lite isolation, version skew, and rollback behavior.

High-risk behavior changes use tdd with explicit failing and passing evidence; the medium metadata task uses acceptance-first validation. Unit, acceptance, regression, parity, integration, and repository-suite evidence match their task surfaces.
Each task identifies the atomic revert scope, the state or behavior to restore, and an isolated or repository-level validation that exercises the rollback.

Task goals and exclusions separate transition repair, registry migration, validator implementation, repository/CI wiring, gate wiring, and release metadata. Shared files are touched for distinct ordered concerns.

Overlapping surfaces are ordered transitively: T-003 depends on T-001/T-002; T-004 and T-005 depend on T-003; T-006 depends on T-004/T-005. No conflicting tasks can execute concurrently under the declared graph.

T-001 preserves the complete diagnostic path from the observed Spec-Passed to Impl-precheck failure, through canonical hash/path mismatch diagnosis and narrow correction, to Red-to-Green regression, adversarial normalization checks, and rollback reproduction.
